hero.heading: Hi! I'm Shameer Ali
hero.description: BS Computer Science at the University of Agriculture, Peshawar. I build end-to-end web apps — React UIs backed by Express APIs and MongoDB.

about.title: MERN STACK DEVELOPER
about.description: I build end-to-end with the MERN stack: schemas and queries in MongoDB, routes and business logic in Express and Node, and fast, maintainable UIs in React. I'm also completing my BS in Computer Science at UAP. My projects span ecommerce flows, dashboards, and booking systems — the same stack from database to deploy. GitHub and my live portfolio are the best place to see the work.

details.title: MINIS E-Commerce
details.description: A modern full-stack e-commerce application built with the MERN stack. It includes user authentication, product browsing, cart & wishlist functionality, secure checkout, and a powerful admin dashboard for managing products, users, and orders. Designed with a scalable architecture and real-world e-commerce features.

details.title: LuxeShop — Premium E-Commerce Experience
details.description: LuxeShop is a full-featured e-commerce web application combining a visually rich shopping experience with an advanced admin dashboard for real-time analytics and business management.

allProjects.title: Parador Hotel Booking
allProjects.subTitle: React Hotel Booking App · Admin + Guest System

details.title: Parador — Hotel Booking Frontend
details.description: A modern single-page hotel booking application built with React, featuring room browsing, cart management, and a complete checkout flow. It includes role-based authentication with guest and admin access, persistent state using Redux Toolkit with redux-persist, and a full admin dashboard for managing rooms and bookings — all running without a backend using localStorage.

details.title: Hope Medical — Doctor Appointment App
details.description: A modern doctor appointment booking web application built with React and Vite, featuring a clean UI, responsive design, and smooth user experience. Users can browse doctors by specialty, search listings, and book appointments with time slots using a simple authentication flow.
details.type: Frontend Web Application (Healthcare / Appointment Booking System)

details.title: DashHub — Business Dashboard Application
details.description: A modern, high-performance business dashboard built with React 19 and TypeScript, featuring role-based authentication, advanced navigation system, interactive data visualizations, and powerful data tables. Designed as a premium UI/UX showcase with real-world admin dashboard functionality.
details.type: Frontend Web Application (Admin Dashboard / SaaS UI)
details.platform: React 19 (Vite), Zustand, React Router 7, TanStack Query

allService.title: Product-grade frontends
allService.subTitle: I ship SPAs with React whenever I want something fast and expressive: reusable components, readable structure, and layouts that behave on phones and desktops.

allService.title: MERN stack builds
allService.subTitle: I've completed the full loop: MongoDB for data modelling and queries, Express and Node for APIs and middleware, and React for interfaces that consume those endpoints cleanly — not mock APIs or UI-only demos.

allService.title: Modern app foundations
allService.subTitle: I care about how a repo is set up: routing, env config, and tooling (Vite today, Next.js when the project calls for it) so the next feature doesn't turn into a rewrite.

allService.title: UX details that ship
allService.subTitle: Small details matter — spacing, type scale, forms that don't fight the user — plus basic accessibility and sane loading/error states.

allExperience.notes: Coursework alongside production-style MERN projects — ecommerce-style apps, dashboards, booking systems — so what I ship in MongoDB, Express, and Node matches how I deliver React on the frontend.

sectionHeading.title: TECH I USE FOR MERN
sectionHeading.intro: The four pieces I reach for daily when building apps from MongoDB — Express — React — Node.

items.name: MongoDB
items.blurb: Schemas, indexes, aggregation, and sane document design so reads and writes stay predictable as apps grow.

items.name: Express.js
items.blurb: REST routes, middleware, validation, and auth-friendly patterns that sit between React and the database.

items.name: React
items.blurb: Component-driven interfaces, hooks, routing, and state that map cleanly to API responses and loading states.

items.name: Node.js
items.blurb: JavaScript on the server — env config, async I/O, and tooling that keeps dev and deploy straightforward.
